resolutionNote: NRSV and rawText, both blocked as of the original 2026-06-28 recording below, were resolved in the full five-translation remediation pass on 2026-07-13. See AUDIT_GOVERNANCE_LEDGER.md's 'Tobit remediation' entry for full detail: NRSV was populated from NRSV-CI.SQLite3 (this project's established primary NRSV source), with the section-header-pollution pattern found and stripped (the same class of bug as the corpus-wide NABRE fix, but in the NRSV column, and specific to this book -- NRSV corpus-wide has not been checked for this pattern in any other book). The one documented internal-order exception (9:3/9:4, where NRSV-CI's own text follows an alternate witness's verse order with a footnoted alternate numbering) was deliberately excluded from the fix and left in the app's existing standard order, matching the precedent already set for Exodus 22:1-4. The rawText anomaly at 10:14 was resolved by removing it entirely: independently confirmed (via KJVA's real chapter-10 verse count, the pre-existing NRSV source-shape classification file below, and content comparison) that this address has no genuine independent content in any of the five translations -- its content was a near-duplicate of verse 12's already-correct text.

trustedClaims.NABRE: trusted_exact_source_collated_2026_07_13 (header-pollution pattern found and fixed, 15 verses, same class as the corpus-wide protocanon fix but this book was outside that effort's scope)
trustedClaims.NRSV: trusted_exact_source_collated_2026_07_13 (populated from NRSV-CI.SQLite3; 168 of 242 source rows corrected, dominated by a section-header-pollution pattern; 9:3/9:4 deliberately excluded as a documented internal-order exception)

rawText.reason: Address 10:14 has no genuine independent content in KJV (KJVA's own chapter 10 ends at verse 12), NRSV (NRSV-CI has no row past 13, confirmed independently and matching this file's own prior NRSV source-shape classification below), NABRE, or DRB (already governed absent per the source-address policy). The rawText content was a near-duplicate of verse 12's already-correct combined farewell scene. Removed rather than reattributed to any translation.

knownAbsencesOrInactiveSourceRows.Rotherham: absent with 0 active rows -- Rotherham never covered the Apocrypha historically, not a gap
knownAbsencesOrInactiveSourceRows.DRBSourceChapter0: one source row is introductory/title material and inactive under policy

allowedClaims: Tobit KJV, NABRE, DRB, and NRSV active lanes are exact-source-collated within their recorded scopes.
Tobit's rawText anomaly is resolved (removed as redundant, not classified as belonging to a translation).
Tobit is fully trusted across all five translation lanes as of 2026-07-13.
forbiddenClaims: Tobit's 9:3/9:4 internal-order exception is a defect -- it is a documented, deliberate textual-tradition feature, not something to 'fix' to match NRSV-CI's raw internal ordering.
This resolution generalizes to any other deuterocanonical book's NRSV column -- the header-pollution pattern found here has only been confirmed in Tobit; other books need their own independent check.
nextRequiredWork: Check other deuterocanonical books' NRSV columns for the same section-header-pollution pattern found here, rather than assuming Tobit was an isolated case.
